creator: markj@FreeBSD.org
text:
This applies also to all of the machine-dependent directories under include, i.e., /usr/include/{i386,amd64,...}.  Moreover, unconditionally creating /usr/include/x86 isn't right on non-x86 systems.

In practice this seems to mostly be a cosmetic issue since include/Makefile creates the directories explicitly.  To fix this problem properly we'd need to have per-arch mtree files.  It's not very clear to me that that's worth the effort.
